hard.next_step: 6.22.26 KK - no response or movement after initial call. Shared followup asking about the RFPs and next steps. Invited to horizon Zycus to send the corporate deck and relevant product information. Mozammil/team to share the eInvoicing RFP or report with Zycus. Set up a 30-minute presales/demo session focused on eInvoicing, eSourcing, and autonomous agent sourcing. Validate Oracle, Coupa, Slack, and Google integration feasibility. Position Zycus as a modular entry point now and broader S2P/AI partner later.
hard.ais_why:
• Riot Games is comparing 'disparate procurement systems' and wants 'clear functional differentiation between intake/orchestration vendors,' with interest in eInvoicing as a standalone module.
• Agentic AI and Merlin are scoped, so AI-enabled orchestration is in scope, but the buyer's stated focus is orchestration fit and module flexibility.
• No agentic discussion or AI sentiment is captured, keeping appetite mid-range and evaluation-driven.
hard.primary_competitor: Riot's own multi-vendor intake/orchestration evaluation (Zycus a late modular entrant)

north_star_verdict.headline: Very early Qualified deal ($120k, eInvoicing entry) that has gone quiet: the only buyer touch is the 11 June intro call with Moz Aslam (Sourcing), and every touch since (12 June email, 22 June Nooks call, 30 June call attempts) is rep-side chasing with no buyer reply. Close date is 340 days out so timing is not the problem; the problem is a single functional contact who has not returned the promised eInvoicing RFP or booked the demo, with no economic buyer, decision maker, or champion mapped.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-11, ~6 days in Qualified (moved Initial Interest to Qualified 2026-06-30 by Alexa Bradley), ~35 days on the opp overall. Close date 2027-06-11 is 340 days out, so long early-cycle runway and no date pressure. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange is the 11 June intro call (12 June auto out-of-office reply is not engagement); rep has attempted contact 12/22/30 June with zero buyer response, so ~25 days of buyer silence. No Formal Eval, Shortlisted, or other submission dates recorded. Not yet 60 days cold, so Slowing rather than Off Track. Pace required: the owner must re-open the Moz Aslam thread and land the eInvoicing RFP + demo to confirm this is a fundable initiative and not a placeholder.
evidence: Buyer silent ~25 days: the last real two-way exchange was the 11 June intro call; the 12 June, 22 June (Nooks could-not-connect) and 30 June touches are all rep-side attempts with no reply (SF activities).

Single-threaded through one functional contact: Moz Aslam (Sourcing, Riot Games) is the only person on the deal; zero OpportunityContactRoles, no economic buyer, decision maker, or champion identified.
Promised buyer deliverable outstanding: Moz agreed his team would share the eInvoicing RFP or report, which has not arrived (Next_Step__c, 2026-06-22).
Pain is qualitative only: disparate procurement systems and manual invoicing named, but no cost, volume, headcount, or cycle-time figure, and no budget owner (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).

metrics.narrative: Riot's articulated problem is fragmented, disparate procurement systems and a desire for clear functional differentiation between intake/orchestration vendors, with manual invoicing driving the eInvoicing interest (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22). No quantified impact was captured on record: no invoice volume, manual-touch time, headcount cost, error rate, or savings target. Because the only substantive interaction (11 June intro call with the relevant stakeholder) was not transcribed into this sweep, the business case remains qualitative. Quantifying the eInvoicing pain (invoices per month, exception rate, days-to-pay) in the promised presales session is the move that would unlock a real value story.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget owner is named anywhere in this sweep: there are zero OpportunityContactRoles, no X10a_Sponsor or budget-approver field value on record, and the only person in contact is Moz Aslam in Sourcing, who reads as a functional evaluator rather than the budget holder. Nothing in the evidence lets us honestly infer who controls the eInvoicing spend, so we do not assert one. For a net-new logo at $120k the funding owner is most likely a procurement or finance leader above Moz; identifying and reaching that person is a ranked move, and until then any commercial conversation is unsupported.
sources: Moz Aslam out-of-office signature: 'Moz Aslam // Sourcing // Riot Games'

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria that surface from the record are: clear functional differentiation between intake/orchestration vendors, eInvoicing available as a standalone module, and integration feasibility with Oracle, Coupa, Slack, and Google (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22). Integration into the existing Coupa/Oracle/Slack/Google stack is a concrete, buyer-relevant test Zycus must pass. These are inferred from the rep's post-call notes rather than a shared RFP; the actual weighted criteria live in the eInvoicing RFP that Moz has not yet sent, so criteria are known in outline but not yet confirmed in the buyer's own words.

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is largely unknown. What the record shows is a single intro call on 11 June followed by buyer silence, so we cannot yet read a committee, an approval chain, or a decision timeline from the call sequence. Riot is evaluating multiple intake/orchestration vendors in parallel and separately running an eInvoicing RFP, which implies a formal sourcing-led process, but no stages, gate dates, or approvers are documented. Getting the eInvoicing RFP and asking Moz to map who scores it and who signs off is what would turn this from a gap into a real process picture.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been touched. The 11 June conversation was about fit and integrations; no MSA, Order Form, SOW, InfoSec, legal, or procurement-channel detail exists on record, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c carries no forcing date. This is entirely stage-appropriate for an early Qualified deal 340 days from close and is not a risk yet. Visibility will come naturally once the eInvoicing scope is validated and the deal reaches commercials; nothing here needs action now.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain Moz Aslam surfaced is real and specific in kind if not in size: Riot runs disparate procurement systems and wants clear functional differentiation between intake/orchestration vendors, with manual invoice processing the trigger for the eInvoicing interest (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22). The pain owner appears to be Moz's Sourcing team, but the executive who feels the cost of that fragmentation has not been identified. The pain is confirmed qualitatively but unquantified, and it sits inside a broader orchestration evaluation where Riot is further along with other vendors, so Zycus's wedge is the eInvoicing module as a fast, low-risk proof of value.

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Moz Aslam (Sourcing, Riot Games) is the sole contact and engaged well enough on 11 June to invite materials and agree to share the eInvoicing RFP, but he has not responded to any follow-up since 12 June, has not returned the RFP, has not booked the demo, and has shown no advocacy or access to power. That reads as a passive, cooling functional evaluator, not a developing champion. With a single unresponsive thread and no introductions upward, the immediate priority is to re-engage Moz directly and, in parallel, open a second thread into Sourcing or Finance so the deal does not live or die on one quiet contact.
sources: SF activities 2026-06-22 Nooks could-not-connect, 2026-06-30 call attempts

competition.narrative: The strongest current competitive fact is Riot's own state of play: it is comparing disparate systems and running an active multi-vendor intake/orchestration evaluation, and Zycus is a late modular entrant proposing eInvoicing as the wedge (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22). Coupa reads as an incumbent/existing system rather than a pure rival, since Zycus was asked to validate Coupa integration feasibility alongside Oracle, Slack, and Google (Next_Step__c 2026-06-22). Globality, GEP, and Ariba are carried from prior-sweep memory with no fresh evidence this run, so they are held at low/dormant threat and not re-ranked. How Zycus wins: land the eInvoicing RFP fast, prove Coupa/Oracle/Slack/Google integration, and use a quick modular win to earn a seat in the broader S2P and agentic-orchestration conversation.
sources: Prior canonical record (living memory): Globality, GEP, Coupa, Ariba

deal_movement.summary: Movement is administrative and rep-side, not buyer-side. Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified on 30 June (Alexa Bradley) and Division moved to New Markets, but the buyer has not re-engaged since the 11 June intro call. Every touch since is an unanswered rep attempt.

items.change: Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified (by Alexa Bradley)
Division reassigned Zycus to New Markets (by Alexa Bradley)
Rep follow-up email asking about the eInvoicing RFP and next steps; no buyer reply
Nooks call to Moz Aslam could not connect
Introductory Call held with Riot Games; buyer invited materials and agreed to share eInvoicing RFP

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is not a named logo but Riot's own advanced, multi-vendor intake/orchestration evaluation, in which Zycus entered late and is trying to win a seat via the eInvoicing module (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22). Coupa is best read as an incumbent/existing system Zycus must integrate with (Oracle, Coupa, Slack, Google integration validation, 2026-06-22), not a confirmed active rival this run. Globality, GEP, and Ariba are carried forward from prior-sweep memory with no new evidence and are held at low/dormant threat, not re-ranked. No new competitor surfaced this sweep.

competitors.name: Riot multi-vendor intake/orchestration evaluation (unnamed vendors)
competitors.quote: Riot Games is comparing disparate procurement systems and wants clear functional differentiation between intake/orchestration vendors, with interest in eInvoicing as a standalone module.
competitors.how_we_win: Enter via a fast eInvoicing module win, then expand to broader S2P and Merlin agentic orchestration once value is proven.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Validate Oracle, Coupa, Slack, and Google integration feasibility.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus to coexist with and complement the existing Coupa/Oracle stack, proving clean integration rather than rip-and-replace.

competitors.name: Globality
competitors.quote: Named as a competitor in prior-sweep memory; no fresh evidence this run.
competitors.how_we_win: Re-confirm whether Globality is truly in the eInvoicing RFP before positioning against it.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: Named as a competitor in prior-sweep memory; no fresh evidence this run.
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on agentic AI (Merlin) and modular eInvoicing speed if GEP re-surfaces in the RFP.

competitors.name: Ariba
competitors.quote: Named as a competitor in prior-sweep memory; no fresh evidence this run.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus modular deployment and AI orchestration against SAP Ariba complexity if it re-surfaces.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is provisional and rests on integration and modularity. Riot wants a clearly differentiated intake/orchestration story, eInvoicing as a standalone module, and clean integration into its existing Oracle/Coupa/Slack/Google stack. Zycus maps well on modularity and AI but has not yet proven the integrations, so those criteria are exposed until demonstrated.

items.grounding_quote: Mozammil/team to share the eInvoicing RFP or report with Zycus.

Pain articulated qualitatively; no invoice volume, cost, or cycle-time figure captured.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified, single-threaded, cooling deal: the win levers are re-engaging the one live contact, breaking single-thread, and landing the RFP to confirm real intent.
flags: Break the single thread: open a second contact in Sourcing or Finance so the deal does not depend on Moz Aslam alone.
Re-engage before it goes cold: the owner should personally re-open the Moz Aslam thread with a specific eInvoicing value hook, not another generic chase.
Qualify real intent: secure the promised eInvoicing RFP and a booked demo to confirm this is a funded initiative versus a placeholder.

items.name: Mozammil Aslam
items.title: Sourcing
items.sentiment: Neutral to cooling. Engaged constructively on the 11 June intro call and invited materials, but has not replied to any follow-up since 12 June and has not returned the eInvoicing RFP.
items.risk: Sole contact and a functional evaluator, not a budget owner; if he stays silent Zycus has no other thread into Riot.
items.source: SF task/activity contact; out-of-office signature 'Moz Aslam // Sourcing // Riot Games' 2026-06-12

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Moz Aslam is the only contact, a Sourcing functional evaluator who engaged once on 11 June then went quiet; no advocacy, no upward access, no delivered commitments. Weak and cooling.
champion_strength.source: SF activities 2026-06-11 to 2026-06-30; OpportunityContactRole (zero roles); Next_Step__c 2026-06-22

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin and Agentic AI are in scope and eInvoicing plus autonomous agent sourcing are the intended demo focus, but the AI story has not yet been put in front of the buyer and no AI appetite was expressed, so it is scoped but not yet landed.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 5.0 / Moderate: AI (Merlin, Agentic AI) is scoped but the buyer's stated focus is orchestration fit and module flexibility, with no agentic discussion captured. Evaluation-driven mid-range appetite; no calls read this sweep to confirm or revise it.

items.detail: No economic buyer or budget owner mapped; only a Sourcing functional contact engaged.
Single-threaded through Moz Aslam; no second stakeholder for resilience.
Buyer silent ~25 days after the intro call; momentum stalled with promised RFP and demo undelivered.
Fit hinges on unproven Oracle, Coupa, Slack, and Google integrations.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: a strong intro call followed by ~25 days of one-way rep chasing and two undelivered buyer commitments.
items: No buyer reply to the 12 June or 22 June follow-ups; 22 June Nooks call could not connect.
eInvoicing RFP promised by Moz's team not received.
Six 30 June opportunity-health call attempts, all rep-side, none connecting.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay / S2P and AI-orchestration vision (iSource, eInvoicing, Merlin, Agentic AI)
scope_change.to: eInvoicing as a standalone modular entry point, broader S2P/AI positioned as a later phase
scope_change.detail: The buyer explicitly wants eInvoicing as a standalone module and the rep is now positioning Zycus as a modular entry point now and broader S2P/AI partner later, narrowing the near-term deal to a single module (AIS_Why 2026-06-11; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22). This is a defensive, phased posture: Riot is committing to a small first step rather than the full suite.

items.action: Owner re-opens the Moz Aslam thread with a specific eInvoicing value hook and a demo slot.
items.trigger: No buyer reply since the 12 June email; 22 June Nooks call could not connect.
items.expected_effect: Restarts a stalled thread and tests whether the eInvoicing initiative is live before it goes cold.

items.action: Reach a second Sourcing or Finance contact at Riot to break the single thread.
items.trigger: Moz Aslam is the only contact; zero contact roles and no backup thread.
items.expected_effect: Adds resilience and a path toward the budget owner if Moz stays quiet.

items.action: Run the 30-minute eInvoicing presales demo and confirm Oracle/Coupa/Slack/Google integration feasibility.
items.trigger: Demo and integration validation were the agreed next step on 11 June but never scheduled.
items.expected_effect: Proves the modular fit and the integrations Riot's criteria hinge on, creating momentum.

items.action: Map the eInvoicing budget owner and ask Moz who scores the RFP and signs off.
items.trigger: No economic buyer or decision maker identified; RFP owner unknown.
items.expected_effect: Surfaces the economic buyer and decision process needed to advance past Qualified.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer asked Zycus to send a corporate deck and product information, then went silent for ~25 days; no Zycus-specific lean, praise, or next-step pull (Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: disparate procurement systems and manual invoicing, with no cost, volume, headcount, or cycle-time figure quantified (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A very early, $120k eInvoicing entry into Riot Games that has already cooled: one good 11 June intro call with Moz Aslam in Sourcing, then ~25 days of one-way rep chasing, no economic buyer or champion, and the promised eInvoicing RFP and demo still undelivered.

win_position.text: Win position is low: this is an early Qualified, single-threaded deal where the only buyer, Moz Aslam (Sourcing), has gone quiet for ~25 days and no budget owner or champion is mapped (SF activities; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).
Zycus is a late modular entrant in Riot's active multi-vendor intake/orchestration evaluation, betting on eInvoicing as a wedge rather than leading the primary decision (AIS_Why 2026-06-11).
Both open commitments point the wrong way for us: the buyer owes an eInvoicing RFP that has not arrived, signalling low urgency (Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).

The one asset is a constructive first call: Moz invited the corporate deck and product materials and agreed to share the RFP, so the door is open if the owner re-engages fast (Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is negative: every touch after 11 June is a rep attempt with no buyer reply, and the 22 June Nooks call could not connect (SF activities 2026-06-22).

The 30 June activity is six internal opportunity-health call attempts (org-chart, decision-maker, multi-thread gaps), confirming the deal team itself flags this as thin and stalling (SF tasks 2026-06-30).
No customer-named dates, no requested next meeting, and only a generic info-sharing stage so far (Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is weak: the buyer promised to share the eInvoicing RFP and has not, and has not booked the agreed demo (Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).
Riot is narrowing to eInvoicing as a standalone module rather than committing to the full S2P/AI scope, a defensive, phased posture (AIS_Why; Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).
Engagement rests on one Sourcing evaluator with no upward advocacy or executive sponsorship (SF OpportunityContactRole zero roles).

deal_risk.text: Single point of failure: if Moz Aslam stays silent Zycus has no other thread into Riot (SF activities; zero contact roles).
No economic buyer or budget owner identified, so there is no confirmation the eInvoicing initiative is funded (SF OpportunityContactRole zero roles).
Scope is contracting to a single module and fit depends on unproven Oracle/Coupa/Slack/Google integrations (Next_Step__c 2026-06-22).

customer_commitment.evidence: Two undelivered buyer commitments (RFP, demo) and ~25 days of silence.

deal_momentum.evidence: All post-11-June touches are one-way rep attempts; internal health flags raised 30 June.

deal_risk.evidence: Single-threaded, no EB, scope narrowing, integrations unproven.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — The buyer explicitly wants eInvoicing as a standalone module and the rep is now positioning Zycus as a modular entry point now and broader S2P/AI partner later, (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)

commentary.win_position: Win 0 is carried by business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — The buyer explicitly wants eInvoicing as a standalone module and the rep is now positioning Zycus as a modular….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 44 (slipping backward): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 44, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
